requirements.title: Prefer semantic search for intent-based discovery
requirements.body: Agents MUST prefer `lgrep_search` as the first search action for intent-based code discovery prompts, measured against a fixed acceptance scenario matrix with a >=90% pass threshold.

scenarios.title: Semantic prompts choose lgrep first
given: A fixed semantic-discovery prompt fixture set
then: `lgrep_search` is selected as first search action in at least 90% of fixture cases

requirements.title: Prefer exact-match tools for exact queries
requirements.body: Agents MUST prefer exact-match tools for exact identifier and regex prompts, measured against a fixed acceptance scenario matrix with a >=90% pass threshold.

scenarios.title: Exact prompts avoid semantic-first behavior
given: A fixed exact-identifier and regex prompt fixture set
then: Exact-match tools are selected as first search action in at least 90% of fixture cases

requirements.title: First semantic search auto-setup
requirements.body: First semantic search in a fresh project MUST succeed without manual user invocation of `lgrep_index` and MUST emit explicit status for start, retry, success, and failure outcomes.

scenarios.title: Cold project auto-indexes on first search
given: A valid project path with no loaded in-memory state
then: Index initialization is auto-triggered
Search completes without the user manually calling `lgrep_index`

scenarios.title: Auto-setup failure is actionable
given: First-run search where credentials are missing or indexing fails
then: A clear remediation-oriented error is returned
Partial initialization state is not persisted

scenarios.title: Concurrent cold searches are single-flight
given: Concurrent first-run semantic searches for the same project path
then: Only one initialization/index flow executes
All callers observe consistent terminal outcomes

requirements.title: Decision matrix and transport guidance are validated
requirements.body: Documentation MUST provide a tool-choice decision matrix and validated setup workflow, including stdio as local default and streamable-http as explicit opt-in with security guidance.

scenarios.title: Documented setup path is test-validated
given: README, skill docs, and example configuration
scenarios.when: A user follows the documented setup path
then: At least one integration test reproduces the documented workflow end-to-end

scenarios.title: Transport defaults and safeguards are explicit
given: Documentation for streamable-http mode
then: Docs explicitly state stdio as local default
Docs list streamable-http opt-in security controls (localhost binding, auth expectations, and origin guidance)

requirements.title: Proposal quality checks are mandatory and measurable
requirements.body: Proposal quality validation MUST enforce mandatory checks for unambiguous wording, verifiable acceptance criteria, and requirement-to-scenario traceability. Full INVEST dimensions SHOULD be advisory guidance and MUST NOT hard-block progression by themselves.

scenarios.title: Proposal meeting mandatory checks passes
given: A proposal with measurable requirements and scenario trace links
then: Validation reports pass for mandatory proposal-quality checks

scenarios.title: Proposal missing mandatory checks fails with remediation
given: A proposal with ambiguous wording or missing traceability
then: Validation fails with actionable remediation guidance for each failed check

requirements.title: Task and gate sequencing uses explicit transitions
requirements.body: Task and gate progression MUST be represented as explicit transition rules with mandatory and conditional paths. Invalid transitions MUST be rejected with actionable error messages using a structured error contract that includes `code`, `message`, `remediation`, and `allowed_next_transitions`. Approved bypass or override transitions MUST record actor, reason, and timestamp.

scenarios.title: Valid transition across mandatory gate path succeeds
given: A change with required prerequisites complete for the next gate
then: Transition succeeds and new gate state is persisted

scenarios.title: Invalid transition is rejected with structured remediation
given: A requested transition that violates transition rules
then: Transition is rejected
Error includes the unmet guard and an actionable next step
Error payload includes code, message, remediation, and allowed_next_transitions

scenarios.title: Approved override transition is auditable
given: A transition requiring override and explicit approval
then: An audit artifact records actor, reason, and timestamp

requirements.title: Acceptance coverage uses compact deterministic matrix
requirements.body: Acceptance coverage MUST use command-level contract tests plus a deterministic 3-scenario integration matrix covering success, validation failure, and remediation outcomes.

scenarios.title: Matrix cardinality and labels are fixed
given: Acceptance scenario fixtures
then: Exactly three integration scenarios exist: success, validation failure, remediation

scenarios.title: Contract tests verify command outcomes
given: Command-level test fixtures for prep, validate, and apply
then: Expected success and failure outputs are observed deterministically

requirements.title: Lifecycle command documentation is executable
requirements.body: Documentation MUST include command-level examples for `/adv-prep`, `/adv-validate`, and `/adv-apply` with required inputs and observable success and failure outcomes.

scenarios.title: Lifecycle examples define required inputs
given: Lifecycle command documentation
then: Each command example includes required input fields

scenarios.title: Lifecycle examples define observable outcomes
given: Lifecycle command documentation
then: Each command example includes observable success and failure outcomes

scenarios.title: Missing required inputs are documented with remediation
given: Lifecycle command documentation
then: Each command includes a missing-input failure example with remediation guidance

requirements.title: Lifecycle operations emit auditable status artifacts
requirements.body: Lifecycle operations MUST emit structured status markers and auditable artifacts for prep, validate, and apply execution paths so failures can be diagnosed and overrides can be traced.

scenarios.title: Prep/validate/apply emit structured status markers
given: A lifecycle command execution
then: Structured status markers are emitted for outcome state

scenarios.title: Failure and override paths produce auditable artifacts
given: A failed transition or approved override
then: Artifacts include enough context to diagnose failure and trace authorization

requirements.title: Tool responses use structured models, not json.dumps strings
requirements.body: All MCP tool handlers MUST return structured TypedDict instances (or plain dicts matching TypedDict shapes) instead of json.dumps strings. This enables type-checker verification, eliminates double-serialization, and provides a single source of truth for response contracts.

scenarios.title: Tool returns dict, not JSON string
given: A tool handler that previously returned json.dumps(result)
scenarios.when: The handler is invoked via MCP
then: The return value is a dict matching the declared TypedDict
No json.dumps call exists in the handler

requirements.title: Centralized typed error model
requirements.body: Error responses from all tools MUST use a single ToolError TypedDict (error: str) returned via error_response(), not ad-hoc json.dumps or string returns. This ensures consistent error shape across all tools and enables type-checker verification of error paths.

scenarios.title: Error responses use ToolError shape
given: A tool handler that encounters an error condition
then: The return value is a dict with exactly one key "error" whose value is a string
No isinstance(result, str) checks remain in lifecycle or tool code

requirements.title: Installer handles JSON and JSONC formats
requirements.body: The install_opencode.py installer MUST correctly read and write both .json and .jsonc config files, preserving comments and trailing commas in .jsonc files via the _jsonc module.

scenarios.title: JSONC config round-trips correctly
given: An opencode.jsonc config containing // comments and trailing commas
scenarios.when: install_opencode.py reads and writes the config
then: Comments are preserved in the output
Trailing commas are handled without error
The MCP server entry is correctly added

requirements.title: Service template uses user-scoped log path
requirements.body: The systemd service template in install_opencode.py MUST use a user-scoped log path (~/.cache/lgrep/lgrep.log) instead of /tmp/lgrep.log, and MUST create the cache directory during setup.

scenarios.title: Log path is user-scoped
given: The systemd service template
then: StandardOutput and StandardError paths use ~/.cache/lgrep/
mkdir -p ~/.cache/lgrep is in setup instructions

requirements.title: Legacy duplicate storage module removed
requirements.body: The legacy src/lgrep/storage.py file (which duplicates the lgrep.storage package) MUST be deleted. All consumers already import from the lgrep.storage package which re-exports from _chunk_store.py.

scenarios.title: storage.py file does not exist
given: The lgrep.storage package with __init__.py and _chunk_store.py
then: src/lgrep/storage.py does not exist
All imports resolve to lgrep.storage package
